BASE.name: ATTACK_VECTOR
BASE.justification: Credentials hardcoded in callout auth headers can be abused via remote callout paths and code exposure scenarios.

BASE.name: ATTACK_COMPLEXITY
BASE.justification: Straightforward to misuse once exposed; no special conditions required.

BASE.name: PRIVILEGES_REQUIRED
BASE.justification: Typically requires limited access (e.g., authenticated user/developer) to reach code paths or obtain secrets.

BASE.name: USER_INTERACTION
BASE.justification: Does not depend on user clicking or special actions once credentials are exposed.

BASE.name: SCOPE
BASE.justification: Impacts remain within the same system scope, enabling unauthorized access using the same credentials.

BASE.name: CONFIDENTIALITY
BASE.justification: Credentials disclosure can enable reading sensitive data from integrated systems.

BASE.name: INTEGRITY
BASE.justification: Credential misuse can enable unauthorized changes/actions.

TEMPORAL.name: EXPLOIT_CODE_MATURITY
TEMPORAL.justification: Well-known weakness; easy to demonstrate with examples.

TEMPORAL.name: REMEDIATION_LEVEL
TEMPORAL.justification: Switching to Named Credentials is typically quick and practical.

TEMPORAL.name: REPORT_CONFIDENCE
TEMPORAL.justification: Static analysis reliably detects 'Authorization' header patterns.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_ATTACK_VECTOR
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Attack remains reachable via network-accessible callouts.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: MODIFIED_CONFIDENTIALITY
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Credentials often grant high-value data access.

ENVIRONMENTAL.name: AVAILABILITY_REQUIREMENT
ENVIRONMENTAL.justification: Operational stability is important but secondary here.
